mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or pinned near zero, the discount rate applied to equities declines and risk appetite expands, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. A negative CPIAUCSL trend strengthens this by preserving real easing and reducing inflation-risk premia.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not above baseline whenever FEDFUNDS is declining and CPIAUCSL inflation is flat-to-down, or if the sign of the return effect is negative in a majority of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income growth, which reduces earnings expectations and depresses SPX returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with a UNRATE rise above 0.3 percentage points in 3 months do not produce lower-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns is not materially above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A broad improvement in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumption and revenue growth, which supports near-term earnings expectations and produces positive SPX returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive changes in RSAFS fail to align with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the relation reverses in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL remains contained, the economy is expanding without immediate inflation pressure, which supports margins and lowers the probability of policy tightening, lifting SPX returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with rising INDPRO and stable CPIAUCSL do not show positive 60-day SPX forward returns, or if the effect disappears once inflation is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals collapsing housing demand and construction activity, which feeds through to weaker cyclicals and lower equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by > 10%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakdown episodes are followed by average positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the negative return frequency is not higher than unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in FEDFUNDS tightens financial conditions through higher discount rates and credit stress, so SPX returns should turn negative over the next month after the hike impulse becomes visible.
failure_condition: Reject if rate-hike episodes do not precede lower 20-day SPX returns, or if returns are not significantly worse than in non-hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: The joint improvement of PAYEMS and falling UNRATE signals genuine labor-market healing, which supports income growth and consumer demand; this should raise SPX returns over the next quarter more than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down state does not outperform baseline on 60-day SPX returns, or if either variable alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision gap in GDPC1 versus prior vintage indicates growth momentum is overstated by real-time data; when that gap is large, subsequent SPX performance should be weaker because markets are pricing in less durable demand growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 vintage growth is revised down by more than 1% relative to the prior release
failure_condition: Reject if large downward GDP revision episodes do not coincide with below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign is not negative in walk-forward evaluation.
